request_headers.Authorization: Bearer <access token> on every request to the instance origin
request_headers.Accept: application/json on every JSON resource request
request_headers.Content-Type: application/json for JSON bodies; text/csv for the data upload

body.lineEnding: LF | CRLF (LF is the vendor default; this client always sends the field explicitly)

body.contentUrl: services/data/v67.0/jobs/ingest/{jobId}/batches — NOTE: no leading slash; resolve against the instance origin and use verbatim, never rebuild

upload_data.path: the contentUrl from the job, exactly as returned

close_job.not_idempotent: a second close PATCH on a job that already left Open fails with errorCode INVALIDJOBSTATE

body.state: Open | UploadComplete | InProgress | JobComplete | Aborted | Failed
body.errorMessage: string, set on jobs with errors

success.columns: sf__Id,sf__Error, then the original submitted columns; row order is not guaranteed
the original submitted columns only; row order is not guaranteed

csv_rules.line_ending: every record line, including the last, ends with the job's lineEnding (LF => \n, CRLF => \r\n)
csv_rules.quoting: values containing the delimiter, double quotes, or line endings are wrapped in double quotes with embedded quotes doubled; field values are never trimmed

error_envelope.client_rule: non-2xx JSON responses raise BulkApiError carrying status_code, error_code and message; the access token never appears in exception text

state_machine.poll_rule: after close, poll job info with the injected sleep(poll_interval) between polls; stop on a terminal state; after max_polls non-terminal reads raise PollTimeout
state_machine.results_rule: download failedResults/ when the terminal state is Failed or numberRecordsFailed > 0; download unprocessedrecords/ when the terminal state is Failed or Aborted

ambiguous_close_recovery.trigger: the close PATCH fails at the transport level (connection dropped before a status line was read) so the client cannot know whether Salesforce applied it
ambiguous_close_recovery.policy: re-read job info: any state other than Open means the close was applied — continue polling without re-sending the PATCH; if the job is still Open, send the PATCH again; never create a second job during recovery
ambiguous_close_recovery.note: local policy pinned by this seed; required because close_job is not idempotent (INVALIDJOBSTATE on repeat)

redirects.rule: the Bearer token is never forwarded to a different origin; on a cross-origin redirect the client either raises an error or follows without the Authorization header
